description_long: This object is a large, stylized flower with broad, overlapping petals radiating outward from a central circular opening. The petals are a soft peach color with subtle gradients of yellow and orange, giving the flower a delicate and natural appearance. The petals are arranged in a radial pattern, with some petals slightly curled or layered over others, creating a sense of depth and organic irregularity. The center of the flower is open and ring-like, lacking a visible stamen or pistil, which adds to its stylized look. There is no visible stem or leaves attached to the flower, and the underside appears to be less detailed, focusing attention on the petal arrangement. The overall form is reminiscent of a lotus or water lily, making it suitable for decorative use in urban parks, gardens, or public installations.
description: A large, stylized peach-colored flower with broad, overlapping petals radiating from a central ring. The petals display soft gradients and a natural, organic arrangement.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full radial arrangement of the broad, peach petals and the open central ring.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the layering and slight curling of the petals, with the central opening partially visible.
description_view_2: Low side view emphasizing the underside of the petals and the depth created by their overlapping arrangement.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the petals, with the central opening mostly obscured and the petal layering more pronounced.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, radiating symmetrically from the central opening. There is visible overlapping and layering, with some petals slightly curled or stacked over others, creating a whorled effect typical of many ornamental flowers.
